name: Roma's White Chocolate Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: White chocolate, 10 oz, broken into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
------------
1. Preheat the oven to 275°F.
2. In a large saucepan, heat heavy cream, milk, and sugar over medium heat until the mixture just begins to bubble around the edges but is not boiling.
3. Add white chocolate pieces and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
4. Combine whole eggs and egg yolks in a large bowl. Slowly pour the hot cream mixture into the eggs in a steady stream, whipping the eggs as you pour.
5. Slice bread 1 inch thick (use stale bread or slice the bread then dry in a 275°F oven for about 10 minutes).
6. Place bread slices in a pan measuring about 9 x 12 inches. Pour half the egg mixture over the bread. Use fingers to press bread into the mix so it absorbs liquid and becomes soggy.
7. Pour remaining mixture over bread. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
8. Remove foil and bake an additional 30 minutes, or until pudding is set and top is golden brown.
9. To make sauce: Bring cream to a boil in a small saucepan. Remove from heat and add white chocolate, stirring until smooth and completely melted.
10. Spoon sauce over bread pudding.
11. Serve warm, spooned right out of the pan, topped with sauce and grated chocolate, or prepare ahead for a polished presentation. Chill bread pudding until firm (6-8 hours). Loosen sides from pan with knife and invert onto cutting surface. Cut into 6 squares, then cut each square in half to make 12 triangles. Place on cookie sheet and heat at 275°F for 15 minutes before serving.
